    Home › Aircraft › Boeing 787 Dreamliner

    Boeing 787 Dreamliner

    Boeing · Widebody · In production

    The aircraft that reinvented long-haul economics.

    Length68.28 m
    Wingspan60.12 m
    Range11,730 km
    MTOW254 t
    Typical seats336
    Built1,200+
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by aircraft.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Seats per metre#8 of 30
    4.92 seats/m typical seats ÷ fuselage length
    Range per tonne#18 of 40
    46.2 km/t maximum range ÷ MTOW in tonnes
    Span-to-length ratio#24 of 43
    0.88 wingspan ÷ fuselage length
    Weight per metre#21 of 43
    3.72 t/m MTOW ÷ fuselage length
    Range in hours#12 of 40
    12.3 h maximum range ÷ cruise speed
    Weight per seat#18 of 30
    756 kg MTOW ÷ typical seats
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The Boeing 787 Dreamliner's wingspan, against things you already know the size of.

    Boeing 787 Dreamliner60.12 m
    Football pitch (width)68 m
    Blue whale25 m
    New Routemaster bus11.2 m
    You (probably)1.75 m
    #11 of 43 by length → #16 by weight → #8 by speed →
    Specification

    The numbers

    Length (787-10)68.28 m
    Wingspan60.12 m
    Height17.02 m
    MTOW254,011 kg
    Range (787-10)11,730 km
    Typical seats (787-10)336 (2-class)
    Engines2 × GEnx-1B or Trent 1000
    First flight15 December 2009
    Delivered1,200+

    Compare it

    Put the Boeing 787 next to something

    vs Ilyushin Il-96 →vs Airbus A380 →
    vs Airbus A350 vs Boeing 777 Compare with anything →
    ← PreviousBoeing 777X Next →Boeing B-52 Stratofortress

    Last verified: 2026-07-14 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    The 787 was Boeing's bet that the future of long-haul wasn't bigger — it was leaner. Half the airframe by weight is carbon-fibre composite, the fuselage is wound as giant one-piece barrels, and the systems run on electricity instead of engine bleed air. The payoff: roughly 20% less fuel than the aircraft it replaced, a cabin pressurised lower and humidified higher, and windows a third larger that dim electronically.

    Its real legacy is the route map. The 787 made thin, ultra-long routes profitable that no bigger jet could sustain — Perth to London, San Diego to Tokyo — opening hundreds of new nonstop city pairs and quietly killing the hub-only model the A380 was built for.

    The programme survived a battery-fire grounding in 2013 and a bruising quality-control crisis in 2021–22. Over 1,200 have been delivered across three lengths; the 787-10 at 68.28 metres is the family's stretch and the 13th-longest aircraft ever built.

    Variants

    The Boeing 787 Dreamliner line

    787-8
    The 56.72 m original, 2011.
    787-9
    The 62.81 m mid-size — the family's best-seller.
    787-10
    The 68.28 m stretch. Spec shown above.
    Operators

    Who flies the Boeing 787 Dreamliner

    The top 10 operators by fleet size, of ≈90 worldwide — the rest are aggregated below. Counts marked ≈ are approximate, verified 2026-07-14.

    UAUnited AirlinesThe world's largest 787 operator and the only US carrier flying all three variants: 12 × 787-8, 54 × 787-9, 21 × 787-10.87✓→ NHAll Nippon AirwaysThe launch customer, and for years the largest operator of the type.86✓→ AAAmerican AirlinesThe world's largest operator of the smallest variant: 37 × 787-8 alongside 33 × 787-9.70✓→ JLJapan Airlines≈50→ QRQatar Airways≈48→ BABritish Airways≈43→ EYEtihad Airways≈43→ ACAir Canada≈40→
    CNHainan Airlines≈38
    TKTurkish Airlines≈30→
    Other operatorsAround 80 more airlines worldwide fly the Dreamliner. Counts are fleet-report approximations, verification pass pending.≈450

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
